title: Forward DNS requests to host local servers
body:
### Feature request description

Use host local DNS server as it is configured in host's `/etc/resolv.conf`.

Right now if `podman` detects `systemd-resolved` (`nameserver 127.0.0.53`) it copies `/run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf` into container.

### Suggest potential solution

Implement an embedded DNS server that forwards requests to the host's DNS server like it's done in docker.

Note docker by default copies `/run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf` too, but they call it legacy and implement desirable behavior for custom networks.

### Additional context

Host DNS servers can be configured to work over HTTPS or TLS and verify record signatures with DNSSEC. Podman extracting just nameservers from `systemd-resolved` and writing them raw into the container's `resolv.conf` undermines privacy and security of the system. It's especially problematic for hosts located in authoritarian countries exercising censorship.

comments.body:
We already have our own dns server which does that (aardvark-dns), like docker it is not used by default network you need to create a custom network.

`podman network inspect` shows you the dns_enabled option which must be true for it to use aardvark-dns, that also only works with the bridge driver.

For rootless pasta/slirp4netns both also proxy the dns top host resolver by default.
I checked shell history and found that after creating new network I forgot to add `--network` to the `run` command when I tested it. I'm sorry.
